title: Collection elements should not be replaced unconditionally
description: Collection elements should not be replaced unconditionallyAnalyze your codeintentionality-clearreliabilityBugsuspiciousWhy is this an issue?Storing a value inside a collection at a given key or index and then unconditionally overwriting it without reading the initial value is a case of a "dead store".This rule detects repeatedly adding an element at the same index or key in a collection or adding identical elements to a set.fruits[1] = "banana"; fruits[1] = "apple"; // Noncompliant myMap.set("key", 1); myMap.set("key", 2); // Noncompliant mySet.add(1); mySet.add(1); // NoncompliantThis practice is redundant and will cause confusion for the reader. More importantly, it is often an error and not what the developer intended to do.Available In:Catch issues on the fly,in your IDEDetect issues i
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.
